title: Spring Kafka
learningObjectives: Configure **@KafkaListener** with **manual** acknowledgements and batch modes
Apply **error handlers**, **retry** topics, and **DLT** publishing patterns
Tune **concurrency** and container properties against **partition** counts
Use **KafkaTemplate** safely with transactions and asynchronous send handling
Integrate **Spring** observability for Kafka clients in production dashboards
Align **Spring** transactional listeners with database consistency strategies

sections.title: Why Spring Kafka matters
sections.content:
**Spring Kafka** is the **on-ramp** most **Java** shops use for **Kafka**. Interviewers expect you to connect **listeners**, **error handlers**, and **concurrency** to the **same** **metrics** you would discuss with raw clients.

Focus on **ack** ordering with **database** transactions and **DLT** hygiene—those separate senior answers from demos.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Spring Kafka
sections.content:
### Day 65 — **Spring** **Kafka**

### 1. Producer side
**KafkaTemplate**, **transactions**, **send** callbacks.

### 2. Consumer side
**@KafkaListener**, **batch** vs **record**, **manual** ack.

### 3. Error handling
**DefaultErrorHandler**, **retry** topics, **DLT** publishing.

### 4. Concurrency
Container **threads** vs **partitions**.

### 5. Observability
**Micrometer** metrics and **tracing**.

### 6. Story
Manual **ack** before DB commit causes **loss**; reorder with **transactional** listener or **outbox**.

sections.title: Basic — Spring Kafka vocabulary
Intermediate — Concurrency vs partitions
sections.output: concurrency should relate to partitions, not infinite threads

sections.title: Advanced — Error handler pseudo-flow
Spring Kafka listener container
sections.description: Factory creates concurrent message listeners with error handler chain.

@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Spring Kafka — concurrency literacy (Day 65 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly**: concurrency should relate to partitions, not infinite threads
hints: Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.
State a **failure mode** (e.g. rebalance, ISR shrink, schema break) tied to your answer.

sections.title: Use cases — Spring Kafka
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Spring Kafka**

### 1) **Order** **service** **@KafkaListener**
**Context:** **Spring** **Boot** **microservice** **consumes** **order** **events** and **writes** **DB**.
**Why Day 65:** **Container** **concurrency** vs **partition** count; **error** **handlers**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Set **concurrency** ≤ meaningful **partition** **parallelism**.
2. Use **DefaultErrorHandler** + **backoff** + **DLQ** **publisher** for **poison** **messages**.
3. **AckMode** **MANUAL** when **commit** must follow **DB** **txn**.
**Failure hook:** **Infinite** **retry** on **deserialization** **error** **blocks** **partition**.

### 2) **KafkaTemplate** **for** **command** **topics**
**Context:** **REST** **controller** **triggers** **async** **workflow** **via** **Kafka**.
**Why Day 65:** **Producer** **config** in **Spring** **must** **mirror** **platform** **standards**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Externalize **acks**, **retries**, ** linger**, **compression** in **config** **server**.
2. **Micrometer** **metrics** for **send** **failures** and **latency**.
3. **Integration** **test** with **EmbeddedKafka** + **Testcontainers** for **realism**.
**Failure hook:** **Blocking** **send** on **metadata** **outage** **exhausts** **tomcat** **threads**.

### 3) **Batch** **listener** **for** **bulk** **indexing**
**Context:** **Elasticsearch** **bulk** **API** prefers **batches** of **records**.
**Why Day 65:** **Batch** **error** **handling** and **partial** **failure** **semantics**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Parse** **batch**; **ack** **offsets** only after **successful** **bulk** **response**.
2. **Split** **failures** into **retry** vs **DLQ** per **record** **key**.
**Failure hook:** **One** **bad** **JSON** **record** **fails** **entire** **batch** without **splitting**.

### 4) **Transactional** **outbox** **relay**
**Context:** **@Transactional** **DB** **write** + **relay** **to** **Kafka** **same** **process**.
**Why Day 65:** **KafkaTransactionManager** + **DB** **tx** **ordering** **must** **match** **design**.
**Implementation outline:**
1. **Chained** **transaction** **manager** only if **ops** **approved** **latency** **cost**.
2. **Idempotent** **producer** as **baseline** even **without** **full** **transactions**.
**Failure hook:** **Partial** **commit** **ordering** → **lost** **events** or **orphan** **rows**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **KafkaTemplate**?
conceptual.answer: Spring **producer** facade with **transaction** support and **callbacks** for **send** results. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **KafkaTemplate**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: **Acknowledgment** manual commit?
conceptual.answer: Call **acknowledge** after successful processing for **at-least-once** control. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

conceptual.question: **ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory**?
conceptual.answer: Configures **concurrency**, **error** handlers, **deserializer** adapters. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.answer: Policies for **retries** and **seek** behavior on failures with backoff and **DLT** support. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

conceptual.question: Why tune **concurrency** vs partitions?
conceptual.answer: Threads should relate to **partition** **throughput** goals—too many waste resources. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

conceptual.question: What is a **rebalance storm** and a common trigger?
conceptual.answer: Many consumers **enter** and **exit** the group rapidly, causing **constant** partition revocation. Common triggers: **handler** time exceeding **max.poll.interval.ms**, **GC** pauses, **too-small** session timeouts, or **buggy** deployment rollouts.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is a **rebalance storm** and a common trigger?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why are **sticky partitioners** relevant for **null keys**?
conceptual.answer: They improve **batching** by keeping **null-key** records on the same partition for a while, improving **throughput** but changing **spread** versus pure round-robin. **Throughput** tuning can accidentally create **hotspots** when misread as random sharding.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why are **sticky partitioners** relevant for **null keys**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// @KafkaListener(topics = "orders", groupId = "g1")** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// spring.json.trusted.packages** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

codeBased.question: Why might **`fetch.max.wait.ms`** reduce empty fetches but raise latency?
codeBased.answer: Brokers **wait** up to this bound to **accumulate** data, reducing **busy** polling. Under **low** traffic it can add **wait** time—pair with **`fetch.min.bytes`** consciously.

codeBased.answer: It enables **idempotent** semantics and may adjust **retries** and **`acks`** expectations—**interviewers** expect you to read **docs** for your **exact** client version rather than memorizing defaults.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** Listener **threads** exhausted.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Thread dump.

**(2) Root cause** Blocking calls in **@KafkaListener**.

**(3) Fix** Offload or increase pool carefully.

**(4) Prevention** Lint blocking calls. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** Listener **threads** exhausted.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Bug:** **Ack** before DB **commit**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Duplication/loss confusion.

**(2) Root cause** Wrong ordering.

**(3) Fix** Transaction sync boundaries.

**(4) Prevention** Tests. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Bug:** **Ack** before DB **commit**.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

wrongAnswers: **Manual** ack not needed with Spring — **Correction:** Often required for DB pairing.
**Default** deserializer trusts all classes — **Correction:** **Security** configs required.
**Spring** manages topic creation always — **Correction:** Needs **KafkaAdmin** + ACLs.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Spring Kafka
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Spring Kafka. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Which statement best describes **@KafkaListener** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **@KafkaListener** belongs to the operational model for **Spring Kafka**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **ConcurrentKafkaListenerContainerFactory** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **ErrorHandlingDeserializer**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **ErrorHandlingDeserializer** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.explanation: **SeekToCurrentErrorHandler** belongs to the operational model for **Spring Kafka**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] You see misconfiguration around **DefaultErrorHandler** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **DefaultErrorHandler** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **ErrorHandlingDeserializer** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **SeekToCurrentErrorHandler**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **SeekToCurrentErrorHandler** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Which statement best describes **DefaultErrorHandler** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **DefaultErrorHandler** belongs to the operational model for **Spring Kafka**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] You see misconfiguration around **KafkaTemplate** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **KafkaTemplate** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **ProducerFactory**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **ProducerFactory** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Which statement best describes **ConsumerFactory** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **ConsumerFactory** belongs to the operational model for **Spring Kafka**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] You see misconfiguration around **retry topic** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **retry topic** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Spring Kafka] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

sections.title: Spring Kafka — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bean | Recall | Recall |
| KafkaTemplate | Producer send | Producer send |
| ListenerContainerFactory | Concurrency + errors | Concurrency + errors |
| Acknowledgment | Manual commit | Manual commit |
| ErrorHandler | Retry + DLT | Retry + DLT |
| KafkaTransactionManager | Tx boundaries | Tx boundaries |
| JsonDeserializer | trusted.packages | trusted.packages |
| Batch listener | List consumer records | List consumer records |
| observationRegistry | metrics traces | metrics traces |
| KafkaAdmin | Topic creation | Topic creation |
| RetryableTopic | Retry + DLT wiring | Retry + DLT wiring |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
